share_source: Alliance for Automotive Innovation, Get Connected EV Quarterly Report (Q1 2025), figures compiled with S&P Global Mobility new-registration data. Confirmed values for CA, DC, WA, CO, OR, NV, NJ, HI, CT, VT, MA, DE, FL from Q1 2025 press release; remaining states estimated from Q4 2024 Get Connected ordering and typical state-level adoption levels.
ports_source: U.S. Department of Energy Alternative Fuels Data Center (AFDC) Station Locator, public Level 2 + DC fast EV charging ports by state (afdc.energy.gov/stations/states), as of Q1 2025.
notes: Ports come directly from AFDC Station Locator state counts as of Q1 2025; totals match anchor values (CA ~65k, NY ~20k, FL ~15k, TX ~12.3k, MA ~11k, WA ~8.6k, GA ~7.5k, CO ~7.5k).
Share leaders (CA 23.7, DC 20.6, WA 19.7, CO 19.2, OR 16.0, NV 15.2, NJ 13.1, HI 11.1, CT 11.1, VT 10.9, MA 10.8, DE 10.2, FL 10.1) are confirmed from the Q1 2025 Get Connected press release.

Remaining state share values are best estimates calibrated against Q4 2024 named values (MD 12.3, NY 11.5, VA 10.8, UT 10.2, MI 10.1+) decremented ~1-2pp for the Q1 2025 sequential drop reported by AAI; bottom-tier states (ND, WY, MS, WV, LA, AL, AR, SD) calibrated to the report's note that two states fell under 2%.
Tesla Model Y is the best-selling EV in essentially every US state in the Q1 2025 timeframe per S&P Global Mobility / Cox Automotive; omitted lower-volume states where the leader is more uncertain.
